Heading: British Columbia Provincial Nominee Program BC PNP International Post- Graduate (IPG) Stream
Description: The British Columbia International Post-Graduate stream is part of the British Columbia Provincial Nominee Program (BC PNP), which is administered by the province of British Columbia. This stream focuses on nominating immigrants who have recently graduated from an eligible British Columbia institution at the post-graduate level.
BenifitsHeading: Benefits of BCPNP International Post-Graduate Stream
BenifitsList1: No Job Offer Required: Candidates do not need a job offer to apply.
BenifitsList2: Nomination Priority: Successful candidates receive an invitation to apply for Canadian permanent residence.
BenifitsList3: No Express Entry Profile Required: No need for an Express Entry profile.
BenifitsList4: Accelerated Processing: Option to apply through Express Entry for faster processing.
EligibilityHeading: Eligibility Criteria for International Post-Graduate Stream
EligibilitySubHead: First you must be eligible with BCPNP Skilled Worker Program
EligibilityList1NestedList1: Must hold a master’s or doctoral (PhD) degree obtained within the past three years.
EligibilityList1NestedList2: Degree must be from an eligible program at a post-secondary institution in British Columbia.
EligibilityList1NestedList3: Doctoral degrees are accepted from any field of study at a public BC institution.
EligibilityList1NestedList4: Master’s degrees must be in natural, applied, or health sciences fields.
EligibilityList1NestedList5: Distance education programs are not eligible.
EligibilityList2: 2. Intent to Live and Work in British Columbia
EligibilityList2NestedList1: Applicants must demonstrate their commitment to residing and working in British Columbia.
EligibilityList2NestedList2: Evidence of this intent can include
EligibilityList2NestedList2NestedList1: Previous or current periods of residence in BC.
EligibilityList2NestedList2NestedList2: Connections to BC through work, study, or family.
EligibilityList2NestedList2NestedList3: Actions taken to permanently settle in BC, such as job search efforts or finding accommodation.
EligibilityList2NestedList2NestedList5: Financial capacity to support oneself in BC.
EligibilityList2NestedList2NestedList6: A plan to obtain or maintain legal authorization to work in BC.
EligibilityList3NestedList1: Candidates must have completed a master’s or doctorate degree from one of BC’s eligible institutions in one of BC’s eligible programs.
EligibilityList3Note: Note: After graduating from an IPG eligible program, candidates are expected to continue residing in BC. Exceptions may be considered in extenuating circumstances where leaving BC was necessary after graduation.
EligibilityList3Remember: Remember that meeting these eligibility criteria does not guarantee an invitation to apply; invitations are based on threshold scores during draws. If your score meets or exceeds the threshold, you’ll receive an invitation to apply from the BCPNP.
ReqExpressHeading: Requirements for the Express Entry Category
ReqExpressDesc: To be eligible for the International Post Graduates (IG) stream, you must qualify under one of the three federal programs. To understand the eligibility criteria for these programs, click on either the:
EmpRequirementsDescription: You do not need a job offer from an employer to apply to this stream.
ApplicationHeading: Application Procedure for British Columbia Provincial Nominee Program BC PNP International Post-Graduate (IPG) Stream
ApplyPoint1List1: The stream is open to new applications at any time.
ApplyPoint1List2: Eligible candidates must submit an application through BC’s online immigration portal.
ApplyPoint1List3: Candidates must specify whether they are applying through the accelerated Express Entry system or the regular Skills Immigration system.
ApplyPoint1List4: The BC International Post-Graduate stream charges a $1,475 CAD application fee per applicant.
ApplyPoint2List1: The stream is open to new applications at any time.
ApplyPoint2List2: If the application is approved, the candidate receives a provincial nomination for permanent residence from BC.
ApplyPoint2List3: Candidates can also request a letter of support for a temporary work permit. This allows them to work in Canada while their permanent residence application is processed.
ApplyPoint2List4: Applying for a work permit with provincial support represents a separate application to IRCC.
ApplyPoint3List1: If the applicant indicated Express Entry on their BC application:
ApplyPoint3List1Nested1: They receive a nomination notification on their IRCC online account.
ApplyPoint3List1Nested2: Accepting the provincial nomination increases their Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score by 600 points.
ApplyPoint3List1Nested3: This virtually guarantees an Invitation to Apply (ITA) for permanent residence in the next Express Entry draw.
ApplyPoint3List1Nested4: After receiving an ITA, the applicant must submit an official application for Canadian permanent residence within the designated timeframe.
ApplyPoint3List1Nested5: Express Entry applications are generally processed within six months.
ApplyPoint4List1: If the applicant indicated the regular non-Express Entry Skills Immigration system on their BC profile, they have six months to submit an online application for permanent residence.

q1: What percentage of your studies must have been completed within Canada to be eligible for the BC PNP?
qa1: At least 50% of your studies must have been completed within Canada to be eligible for the BC PNP?
q2: Are distance education learning programs, whether from abroad or within Canada, considered eligible for the BC PNP?
qa2: No, distance education learning programs, whether from abroad or within Canada, are not eligible for BCPNP.
q3: Is there an exception to the eligibility criteria regarding distance education due to the impact of Covid- 19? If so, what is it?
qa3: Because of the Covid-19 pandemic, online coursework completed between January 2020 and June 2023 as a component of a qualifying program of study for the BC PNP while residing in Canada is deemed eligible.
q4: Are educational credentials granted by campuses of Canadian institutions located outside of Canada considered eligible for the BC PNP?
qa4: No, the educational credentials granted by campuses of Canadian institutions located outside of Canada considered eligible for the British Columbia Provincial Nominee Program International Graduate Stream application.
q5: What is the time limit for submitting registration to the British Columbia Provincial Nominee Program BC PNP International Post-Graduate (IPG) Stream?
qa5: You are required to apply for registration with the BC PNP within three years of your graduation date.
